behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counting + threshold comparison) inside one analyzer, with no required module handoff or external I/O. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System/Agentic are disqualified because behavior text is limited to configured word lists and computed sentiment from that deterministic analyzer.
behaviors.test_description: Call the keyword-frequency analyzer function directly with table-driven inputs built only from configured positive/negative words, and assert exact sentiment outputs at representative thresholds.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on content quality of generated reasoning (phrase grounding vs generic text). A stubbed analyzer would always pass and hide real model failures (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP). REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because behavior explicitly evaluates explanation quality/content, not just routing or API wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real explanation analyzer on several reviews containing distinctive phrases; assert the explanation includes concrete phrase-level grounding from each review and fails if output is generic boilerplate across cases.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic policy logic inside the keyword-frequency analyzer (tie-break decision under mixed counts). No real infrastructure or model output is needed. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because behavior text targets the configured tie-break rule itself, not inter-module state handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test the analyzer with mixed-keyword reviews that land on tie and near-tie boundaries, varying configuration to confirm the configured tie-break rule is applied exactly.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Detecting sarcasm/intent is model judgment, not deterministic wrapper behavior. Mocked outputs would miss real misclassification risk (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because behavior text requires semantic intent detection despite contradictory surface words, which only a real model call can validate.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on sarcasm-focused fixtures and assert explanations explicitly indicate negative intent and cite sarcastic cues; repeat over multiple samples to detect unstable or surface-word-positive failures.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-component coherence where one output is deterministic (keyword score) and the other depends on real generated content; real failures include direction mismatch even when wiring is correct. Per INTEGRATION-BRAKE(d), LLM-involved handoff correctness is governed by model decision, so Integration is too low unless content is stubbed (which would hide mismatch). REJECTION-GATE: Workflow is disqualified because behavior describes one model generation step plus response assembly, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the full service endpoint with the real explanation analyzer enabled; assert response contains both fields in one payload and explanation sentiment direction aligns with returned score across a balanced set of positive/negative/mixed reviews.
